title: Garnished Algerian Sloe-Berries Tagine
description: A flavorful and aromatic Algerian dish featuring sloe berries, perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay tagine or heavy pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the ground cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper and stir for 1 minute.
Add the sloe berries, lemon juice, and honey and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the sloe berries are tender and the liquid has thickened.
Garnish with fresh herbs, such as parsley or cilantro, and serve hot over couscous or rice.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional fresh herbs if desired.
Adjust seasoning as needed before serving.
Let the tagine rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs and a side of couscous or rice, if desired.
